Figure 1

Mei-Ju May Chen (mjmchen[at]ntu.edu.tw)

2026-03-16

Load libraries

library(Seurat)
library(dplyr)  
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(openxlsx)
library(ggpubr)
library(RColorBrewer)

Color codes

# Plot the features and scatterplot with correlation and p-value
major_celltype_color = c("#FC4E07","#E7B800","#00AFBB",brewer.pal(n = 9, name = "Set1")[c(4:5,7,2,8:9)], "lightblue")
minor_celltype_color = c(brewer.pal(n=5, "Set2"), "#E7B800", brewer.pal(n=4, "Set1"))

Load data

rds_path <- "data-to-path"
ob <- readRDS(rds_path)
# Quick overview
ob
## An object of class Seurat 
## 30203 features across 27009 samples within 2 assays 
## Active assay: RNA (28203 features, 0 variable features)
##  2 layers present: counts, data
##  1 other assay present: integrated
##  3 dimensional reductions calculated: pca, tsne, umap

Figure 1C

labels = rev(levels(factor(as.vector(ob$Cell.Type.L2))))
ob$Cell.Type.L2 = factor(ob$Cell.Type.L2, levels = labels[c(1:2,6,3:5,7:10)])
DimPlot(ob, group.by = "Cell.Type.L2") + scale_color_manual(values = major_celltype_color) +
  DimPlot(ob, group.by = "donor")

Figure 1C

Figure 1D

ob_disease <- SplitObject(ob, split.by = "Sample.type")
features = c("RPS4Y1", "EIF1AY", "USP9Y",  "UTY",    "KDM5D",  "RPS4X",  "USP9X",  "EIF1AX", "KDM6A",  "KDM5C",  "RPLP0")
a = rev(levels(ob$Sample.type))[1]
#for(a in rev(levels(ob$Sample.type))){
  ob_list = list()
  ob_list <- SplitObject(ob_disease[[a]], split.by = "donor")
  dotplots <- list()
  orderlist = names(ob_list)
  orderlist = rev(orderlist)
  # Iterate through each split object and create a DotPlot
  dotplots=list()
  for (i in orderlist) {
    # Create DotPlot for the current split object
    # Create DotPlot for the current split object
    # Add cell counts to Cell.Type labels
    ob_list[[i]]@meta.data$Cell.Type.L2
    cell_counts <- ob_list[[i]]@meta.data %>%
      group_by(Cell.Type.L2) %>%
      summarise(count = n()) %>%
      mutate(Cell.Type.L2 = paste0(Cell.Type.L2, " (N=", count, ")"))
    
    # Update the Cell.Type factor levels in the metadata
    levels(ob_list[[i]]@meta.data$Cell.Type.L2) = cell_counts$Cell.Type.L2
    ob_list[[i]]@meta.data$Cell.Type.L2 = factor(ob_list[[i]]@meta.data$Cell.Type.L2, levels = rev(levels(ob_list[[i]]@meta.data$Cell.Type.L2)))
    # Create DotPlot with updated Cell.Type labels
    p <- DotPlot(ob_list[[i]], features = features, group.by = "Cell.Type.L2", scale = F, 
                    col.min = 0, dot.min = 0) +
      theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 8)
      )       
    
    # Store the plot in the list
    dotplots[[i]] <- p
  }
  # Combine all DotPlots into one plot layout
  combined_plot <- wrap_plots(dotplots, nrow=4)
  # Save the combined plot as a PDF
  #pdf(file = file.path(resPath, paste0("F22_DotPlot_", a, ".pdf")), width = 8, height = 12)
  print(combined_plot)

Figure 1D 

  #dev.off()
#}

Figure 1E

ob_loy = SplitObject(ob, split.by = "LOY.status")
x_limits <- c(-0.4,0.9)
# Save the RidgePlot for ROY as a PDF
# pdf(file = file.path(resPath, "F23_RidgePlot_Y_Score1_ROY.pdf"), width = 12, height = 5)
RidgePlot(ob_loy[["ROY"]], features = "Y.score", group.by = "Cell.Type.L2") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = alpha(major_celltype_color, 0.5)) +
  xlim(x_limits) + 
  NoLegend()

Figure 1E 

# dev.off()

# Save the RidgePlot for LOY as a PDF
#pdf(file = file.path(resPath, "F24_RidgePlot_Y_Score1_LOY.pdf"), width = 12, height = 5)
RidgePlot(ob_loy[["LOY"]], features = "Y.score", group.by = "Cell.Type.L2") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = alpha(major_celltype_color, 0.5)) +
  xlim(x_limits) + 
  NoLegend()

Figure 1E 

#dev.off()
